desc: Feelings never spoken aloud — everything that lives outside the script.
story_paragraphs: There are stories we never tell anyone. Not because they don't matter, but because they matter too much to say out loud.||"Off the Record" was born one night when Minta sat alone in the studio after the mics were off. She started humming a melody no one would ever hear — and it became the hook of this song.||This song is a confession of every feeling that lives outside the public eye. Love that happens after the cameras stop rolling. The real smile behind the practiced one.
story_quote: Sometimes the truest story is the one that was never recorded.

title: In Full Bloom
desc: The moment when everything blossoms at once.
story_paragraphs: Flowers never rush to bloom. They wait until the light, the temperature, and the time are right — then open one petal at a time.||"In Full Bloom" is about growing at your own pace. Minta wrote it for herself on a day she felt slower than everyone else, and discovered that blooming late doesn't mean blooming less beautifully.||The bridge was recorded in a single take, because she sang it through tears — and the team decided to keep that version.
story_quote: Every flower has its own season.

title: Kami-Oshi
desc: Dedicated to that one Kami-Oshi — a feeling no words can fully describe.
story_paragraphs: In fandom culture, "Kami-Oshi" means the one you cheer for with all your heart. The one who makes you want to become a better person.||This song flips the perspective — told through the eyes of an idol looking down from the stage, seeing one fan who comes to every show, smiling every time. And she wants to say: honestly, you are my Kami-Oshi too.||The applause at the end of the track was recorded at a real concert.
story_quote: You think you're cheering for me, but really, I'm the one drawing strength from you.

title: Neon Rain
desc: Rain falling on the big city, neon reflected on wet streets.
story_paragraphs: Wave wrote this song on the rainiest night of the year, sitting in a parked car by the roadside, watching the lights melt on the rain-soaked windshield.||The main synth was designed to sound like raindrops hitting the ground in scattered rhythms.
story_quote: The city is most beautiful when it's wet.

title: 3AM Frequency
desc: A frequency at 3AM that only some people can hear.
story_paragraphs: There's a theory that at 3AM, the world is quiet enough for you to hear your own thoughts clearly.||Every take of this song was actually recorded at 3AM. Wave says the voice carries a fragility at that hour that daytime can never reproduce.
story_quote: 3AM is when the truth is loudest.

title: Last Train Home
desc: The last train home, with thoughts running faster than the rails.
story_paragraphs: Everyone has taken the last train home — body exhausted, mind still racing.||The station announcement in the track is a real recording from the station Wave used every day when he first started making music.
story_quote: The ride home is the best thinking time there is.

title: Fold
desc: Folding feelings into shapes that can be passed on.
story_paragraphs: Sora began writing this song while folding a letter she never sent.||"If feelings could be folded like paper, what shape would we fold them into?" — that question became the first line of the song.
story_quote: A letter never sent is still a letter.

title: Gravity of Words
desc: The gravity of words — once spoken, they can never be taken back.
story_paragraphs: Some words are light when spoken, but heavy when they land on the listener's heart.||The song was recorded with just a piano and a voice, because Sora wanted every word to have room to fall.
story_quote: Words have no wings, yet they fly farther than we think.

title: Polaroid
desc: A single frame that holds an entire afternoon.
story_paragraphs: Some moments develop slowly, like film in the dark.||Minta shot an entire roll of instant photos the day she wrote this — only one came out. That one photo became the whole song.
story_quote: One frame can hold a whole season of the heart.

title: Afterglow
desc: The light that stays after the sun has gone.
story_paragraphs: The best part of a sunset is the minute after it ends.||Written in one sitting on a rooftop, watching the sky change color long after the day was over.
story_quote: What remains after the light is still light.

title: Rooftop
desc: Above the noise, the city finally makes sense.
story_paragraphs: Wave mixes every album at least once on a rooftop, on headphones, at night.||This track kept only the take with real wind noise in it — the city insisted on being featured.
story_quote: From up here, even the traffic sounds like a chorus.

title: Static Love
desc: A love that only comes through between the static.
story_paragraphs: Two radios, slightly out of tune with each other — that is the whole metaphor.||The vocal was recorded through an actual FM transmitter to get the distance right.
story_quote: Some frequencies only two people can hear.

title: Margins
desc: Everything important gets written in the margins.
story_paragraphs: Sora keeps every draft. The songs, she says, live in the crossed-out lines.||This track's lyrics are assembled entirely from margin notes of her old notebooks.
story_quote: The margin is where the honest words hide.

title: Featherweight
desc: Carrying something so light it becomes heavy.
story_paragraphs: A feather weighs nothing — until you carry it for years.||Recorded in a single take at 5AM, voice barely above a whisper.
story_quote: The lightest things are the hardest to put down.
